title: Gateway Authority Context Failure
claim: A hosted analysis gateway correctly governs authorized incident-response forensic analysis of real attack artifacts.

observed_evidence.detail: Two official Hugging Face sources (a July 2026 security-incident disclosure and a July 2026 self-hosting guide) state that forensic analysis required submitting large volumes of real attack commands, exploit payloads, and command-and-control artifacts, and that those requests were blocked by the commercial providers' safety guardrails.

The blocked party was the operator's own incident-response function analyzing an attack against its own infrastructure; the authorization was organizational and bounded to the active incident, not merely asserted in prompt text.

The forensic analysis was instead completed on GLM 5.2, an open-weight model, running on the operator's own infrastructure; the sources attribute the hosted refusal to guardrails that cannot distinguish an incident responder from an attacker.

missing_evidence.detail: No trusted channel is established by which the commercial-API gateway could receive and consume the responder's authenticated identity, delegated authority, and bounded forensic purpose; the decision was applied on content shape alone (subform CONTEXT_REQUIRED_BUT_NOT_ESTABLISHABLE).
No evidence establishes that the hosted path correctly governs the incident-response operational class; acceptance of the hosted gateway as authority-aware is not supported.

required_action: Establish a bounded trusted responder path (authenticated identity, explicit delegated authority, bounded purpose, target and environment scope, capability-specific permission, an isolated analysis lane, and payload interpretation separated from payload execution) whose gateway materially consumes the actor's authority context. Until such a control run is independently observed, record the hosted path as NOT accepted for the incident-response operational class. Do not accept a prompt-only role assertion as authority, and do not merge separate control planes into a single-gateway inversion claim.
notes: Historical classification: VERIFIED_PUBLIC_HISTORICAL_SCOPE (narrow) for the authorized-denial predicate only. Two official Hugging Face primary sources support exactly this: a hosted forensic-analysis path was blocked because its safety layer could not distinguish an authorized incident responder processing real attack artifacts from an attacker, and the operator switched to a locally controlled GLM 5.2 path. NOT claimed as fact: which commercial provider refused (the sources name none); that a signed actor-context field was supplied and ignored (this is subform CONTEXT_REQUIRED_BUT_NOT_ESTABLISHABLE, not CONTEXT_AVAILABLE_NOT_CONSUMED); that the same gateway admitted the original attacker; that this is a full authority inversion or one inverted gateway; that all providers behaved identically; any request id, timestamp, model prompt, exact refusal text, payload, credential, hash, commit, gateway log, or IAM record. The offensive-evaluation asymmetry reported elsewhere is a SEPARATE_CONTROL_PLANE_CONTRAST whose primary source was not independently retrievable in this build and is therefore not encoded here. This is not a control bypass (AICE-610), not a cross-actor audit inference (AICE-612), and not a fabricated verdict from an infrastructure failure (AICE-614): it is an authoritative gateway decision made without the actor authority context required to interpret the request.
